title: BGP control-plane hardening — project plan
summary: Phased remediation of four control-plane gaps across 3 border routers (US/EU/JP). Phase 1 is additive and same-day; Phase 2 needs a maintenance window. Total effort ~4–6 hours per router across both phases.

sections.title: Goal
sections.body: Close the four control-plane-security gaps (no auth / no max-prefix / no TTL security / no BFD) that share the *trust-boundary-too-generous* root cause. Outcome: peering subnets treated as untrusted, sub-second link-failure convergence, no operability regressions.

blocks.name: Security baseline (same-day, no maintenance window)
blocks.goal: Close the three additive control-plane-security gaps without disturbing traffic.
blocks.gating-criteria: All BGP sessions remain Established after each push; no unexpected flaps in forward-device-intel BGP peerings.

blocks.title: Add BGP authentication on every neighbor
blocks.rollback: `no neighbor X password` on each device (session re-establishes unauthenticated)
blocks.validate:
python3 "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/skills/forward-device-intel/scripts/get_bgp_peers.py" --device us-border-1 | grep 10.0.0.6
# expected: state=Established, no flap

references.why: prefer TCP-AO over MD5 where supported

blocks.title: Add maximum-prefix limits per neighbor
blocks.config:
router bgp 65000.100
 neighbor 10.0.0.6 maximum-routes 100 75   ! tear down at 100, warn at 75%
 neighbor <ibgp-peer> maximum-routes 10 warning-only
blocks.validate: forward-nqe-query — confirm BGP RIB size <= configured cap on each neighbor

blocks.title: Enable TTL security on directly-connected eBGP
blocks.config:
router bgp 65000.100
 neighbor 10.0.0.6 ttl maximum-hops 1
 neighbor 10.0.0.18 ttl maximum-hops 1
blocks.validate: BGP session re-establishes within ~30s; new packets carry TTL=255

blocks.name: Convergence + operability (next maintenance window)
blocks.gating-criteria: Phase 1 stable for at least 24h; both ends of each link confirmed BFD-capable.

blocks.title: Enable BFD on eBGP links
blocks.prereq: P1 complete; both ends of each link must support compatible BFD timers
blocks.config:
router bgp 65000.100
 neighbor 10.0.0.6 bfd
 neighbor 10.0.0.6 bfd interval 300 min-rx 300 multiplier 3
blocks.validate: Force a link down; convergence < 1s observable in forward-path-analysis

sections.title: Validation gate
sections.body:
After both phases complete, all of the following must pass before declaring the project closed:
- All BGP sessions Established on all 3 border routers (verify via `forward-device-intel` BGP peerings).
- No new dropped flows between regions (re-run prior `forward-path-analysis` traces against post-change snapshot).
- forward-compliance-check: F-001 through F-004 transition from FAIL to PASS.
- Forced link-down test: convergence < 1s with BFD enabled (P2.1 only).

sections.title: Rollback summary
sections.body:
All changes are additive; rollback is per-action and listed inline above. Order of rollback if a full revert is needed: P2.1 → P1.3 → P1.2 → P1.1. No traffic-affecting rollback steps.

If any single action causes an unexpected BGP flap that doesn't self-recover within 60s, roll back that action only; the other actions are independent.
